core_mechanism: iterative strong-disorder decimation of a Markov jump process (master equation): repeatedly eliminate the state with the largest total exit rate and renormalize the effective transition rates among surviving states, with the multiplicative rate structure driving the distribution of log-rate barriers to broaden without bound toward a strong-disorder fixed point, making the coarse-grained description asymptotically exact at long times
brief_reason: The abstract defines a strong-disorder renormalization on any master equation that iteratively removes the configuration of highest exit rate to obtain renormalized transition rates whose multiplicative structure broadens the exit-barrier distribution, so the scheme becomes asymptotically exact at large time scales.
